---
title: DDBJ
alias: &ALIAS ddbj
href: https://www.ddbj.nig.ac.jp/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The DNA Data Bank of Japan (DDBJ) is a member of the International Nucleotide Sequence Database Collaboration (INSDC), providing nucleotide sequence data and annotation services.

## Key Features

- Part of INSDC alongside NCBI and EMBL-EBI, ensuring worldwide synchronization of sequence records
- Accepts submissions of nucleotide sequences, raw reads (DRA), and functional genomics data (GEA)
- Provides ARSA, BLAST, and other bioinformatics search and analysis tools
- Hosts the Japanese Genotype-phenotype Archive (JGA) for human genomic data with controlled access
- Supports MetagenomeDB and microbiome data standards for environmental sequence submissions

## Use Cases in Research

- Submitting and retrieving Asian cohort sequencing data and microbial genome assemblies
- Cross-referencing INSDC records to validate genome annotations for pathogen characterization
- Accessing raw sequencing reads from DRA for metagenomics re-analysis pipelines
- Using JGA for controlled-access human genetic studies relevant to infectious disease
- Ensuring global data availability and redundancy for nucleotide sequence records

## Getting Started

Visit [DDBJ](https://www.ddbj.nig.ac.jp/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
